title: Jataka Tales: The Monkey King (Mahakapi Jataka)
description: The Buddha in a previous life as a monkey king stretches his body as a bridge so his troop can escape across a river. A parable of self-sacrifice carved at every major Buddhist temple in Southeast Asia.
introduction: The Buddha in a previous life as a monkey king stretches his body as a bridge so his troop can escape across a river. A parable of self-sacrifice carved at every major Buddhist temple in Southeast Asia.

translation.text: The monkey king saw his people trapped between the hunters and the river. He stretched his body from one bank to the other and said: 'Walk across me.' The monkeys crossed to safety. But the king's back was broken. The king of the humans wept and said: 'You have given your life for your people. You are a greater king than I.'
